title: RUBEANIC ACID AND ITS DERIVATIVES AS COLORIMETRIC REAGENTS. PART III. NN'-DI-β-HYDROXYETHYL- AND NN'-DI-isoAMYL-RUBEANIC ACIDS
author: J. XAVIER
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The effect of substitution in rubeanic acid on its reactivity as a reagent has further been investiga­ted by examining the behaviour of (I)  NN ’-di- β -hydroxyethyl- and (II)  NN '-di-isoamyl-rubeanic acids. Substitution of a hydrogen atom by a hydroxyl in the ethyl radical of diethylrubcarric acid increases the solubility of the product and of its metallic complexes (e.g. of copper) in certain solvents. Di-isoamyl­rubeanie acid behaves like other disubstituted alkyl derivatives. Both the reagents, if used in caustic alkali solution, retard the colour development, as in the case of the diethyl derivative (of. Part II, this  Journal,  1958, 35, 589). This retardation effect is more pronounced with the increase in the chain length of the substituent. Methods for the spectrophotometric determination of copper (using II), cobalt (using II), nickel (using II), palladium (using I & II) and ruthenium (using I) have been described.
